question.text: When an option writer (seller) receives premium, the accounting entry is:

answer.text: Debit: Bank/Broker Account; Credit: Option Premium Received Account (Liability)
Debit: Bank Account; Credit: Revenue Account (immediately recognized as income)
Debit: Margin Account; Credit: Option Premium Account
No entry until the option expires

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Premium received by the option writer is initially recorded as a liability (obligation exists until expiry). Entry: Debit: Bank/Broker Account (cash received) Credit: Option Premium Received Account (Liability)
On expiry, if the option expires worthless, this liability is transferred to income. If exercised, the settlement amount is adjusted against this liability.
**The Trap:** Premium is NOT immediately income - it is a liability because the writer has an obligation. Income recognition happens only on expiry/exercise.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The asymmetric accounting treatment of option buyers vs sellers is a key concept.
**Key Takeaway:** Option premium received = liability initially. Income recognized only on expiry/exercise. Not immediate revenue.
